steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize the order in which the supercontinents formed. The notes give two data points: Ur formed ~3.1 billion years ago; Pangaea formed ~300 million years ago. The correct answer must explicitly compare the two and indicate sequence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Pangaea formed long after the supercontinent Ur': directly compares both supercontinents and states which came first, satisfying the goal of emphasizing formation order. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Forming and breaking apart over hundreds of millions of years...': describes the supercontinent cycle in general but never names Ur or Pangaea or indicates which formed first. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Ur and Pangaea were both supercontinents, single landmasses made up of most or all of Earth's continents': mentions both supercontinents but only defines what a supercontinent is — no sequence information. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Ur formed about 3.1 billion years ago but eventually broke apart': covers only Ur and omits any comparison with Pangaea, so order between the two is never established. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
